subcodes.title: Words spoken on 1916-08-10 in Haifa
subcodes.first_line: The spirit, in its realm of abstraction, is wholly independent of the body. It possesseth all attributes and, of itself, without need of the body, doth see and hear [3.5s]
subcodes.first_line_orig: روح در عالم تجرد از بدن مستغنی است.  دارای جمیع شئون است.  به نفسه بدون احتیاج بدن می بیند، می شنود
subcodes.subjects: @existence; immortality of the human soul, @relationship of the soul to the body, @mind as intermediary between soul and body, @materialism; material vs. spiritual civilization, @immateriality; ineffability of soul; spirit
